input_refs.hypothesis: A full-screen subscribe popup overlays the hero on load across the homepage and PDPs, taxing the first impression before a visitor sees the product or value proposition.
input_refs.primary_change: Switch the email popup to fire on exit-intent or after ~20s/scroll, and keep the reward offer; never overlay the hero on first load.

input_refs.hypothesis: The Organic Sleep Tea PDP has a review widget but no visible review count, rating, organic-certification badge, or guarantee near the buy box, so a premium-priced product lacks reassurance at the decision point.
input_refs.primary_change: Surface star rating + review count, an organic/quality badge, and a satisfaction guarantee directly under the price and add-to-cart.

input_refs.hypothesis: Collections list teas by name with no benefit filters, asking shoppers to decode products rather than shop their goal (sleep, digestion, immunity).
input_refs.primary_change: Add benefit chips/filters ('For sleep', 'For digestion', 'For immunity', 'Daily ritual') and benefit-led section headers to the collection.

input_refs.hypothesis: Teas and accessories (mug, infuser, tea bags) are sold separately; the brand's 'ritual' positioning is a natural fit for a starter kit that lifts basket size.
input_refs.primary_change: Create a 'Zen Ritual Starter Kit' bundling a featured tea + mug + infuser at a modest bundle discount, merchandised on home and collections.

input_refs.hypothesis: The PDP has no cross-sell near the buy box, missing the obvious accessory and sampler attach for a ritual product.
input_refs.primary_change: Add a 'Complete your ritual' module under add-to-cart suggesting an infuser, mug, or a complementary sampler.

input_refs.hypothesis: Tea is a repeat-consumption ritual product, but PDPs offer only one-time purchase, leaving recurring revenue and retention on the table.
input_refs.primary_change: Add a subscribe-and-save option (e.g. 10% off, monthly/bi-monthly) to consumable tea PDPs with easy management.

input_refs.hypothesis: The Ambassador Program is buried in the footer and never surfaced at purchase or post-purchase, so an existing loyalty asset goes unused.
input_refs.primary_change: Promote the Ambassador Program in the post-purchase flow, account area, and a homepage band, with a clear reward and share path.

input_refs.hypothesis: High-intent functional searches ('best organic tea for sleep', 'tea for heartburn') have no dedicated landing pages; the benefit content is buried in product copy and blog posts.
input_refs.primary_change: Create benefit landing pages (sleep, digestion, immunity) with the relevant teas, proof, FAQs and internal links from blog content.

evidence.store_profile_ref: proof:founder Jesse Rojas story; content_theme:tea education

input_refs.hypothesis: The founder/family story and tea-education content build trust but do not route readers to products or a brand-story shopping path.
input_refs.primary_change: Add product modules and 'shop the story' CTAs into the founder post and education articles, linking to relevant teas and samplers.

input_refs.hypothesis: Pages fire 200+ requests with ~20 console errors from failed third-party (shop.app) scripts, and the best-sellers collection took ~10s to load in capture — a real speed and reliability drag on conversion.
input_refs.primary_change: Audit and lazy-load imagery, defer/remove failing third-party scripts, and compress hero/product images to cut requests and load time.

input_refs.headline: Zen Rojas audit — premium tea, but the path from intent to ritual leaks

themes.title: The first impression fights the shopper
themes.summary: A full-screen email popup blocks the hero on load and PDPs lack visible social proof, so a premium-priced catalog loses trust and momentum at the decision point.

themes.title: The ritual positioning is under-monetized
themes.summary: Tea, accessories and samplers are sold separately with no bundles, cross-sell, or subscribe-and-save — leaving basket size and recurring revenue on the table.

themes.title: Functional demand and content go uncaptured
themes.summary: Benefit-led navigation, intent landing pages, and commercialized founder/education content would convert search and story into shopping; meanwhile page weight drags speed.
